title: The Original Under Cabinet Jar Opener - Effortless for Weak Hands & Seniors with Arthritis - Open Any Size Jar & Bottle - Made in USA
description:
About this item
Easy & Effective: with the lid opener mounted securely, our proven v-shape method grips the lid using its sharp dual Carbon Steel Teeth, allowing the opener to do the work rather than your hands and wrists.
Opens Any Lid: The Grip opens any jar lid or bottle cap effortlessly. Invented by our grandfather, it’s been helping folks make life easy in the kitchen again for over 40 years. Same design. Same quality materials. Still never fails.
It Works: Unlike other jar openers, The Grip never fails. Rubber jar openers slip and strain, hand held jar and bottle openers are ineffective and take up drawer or counter space, Robotic jar openers are bulky, expensive, and malfunction. And any other under cabinet can opener will not last.
Making The Kitchen Easy: Everyone has trouble with lids sometimes, but our lid opener is especially a lifesaver for those with weak hands, arthritis, aging, and seniors. It’s a kitchen gadget essential for any home, and make wonderful gifts for grandma, mom, friends, Christmas, or those with a disability.
Support Small Business: Family-owned and operated, we’ve been Made in the USA since our grandfather invented the under cabinet grip jar opener in 1977. Our can opener has stood the test of time. It continues to be crafted by American workers, made with premium materials and the highest quality standards on the market.
reviews_5s:
Love this under cabinet jar opener. It is extremely easy to install and works beautifullyThis is a perfect tool, if you have a place to install it. So far it has opened everything I tried, and it is always ready, no need to look through the junk drawer to find it. Once installed, it doesn't even show.Love this product. Very easy to install under cabinet and easily opens jar and bottles of all sizesWife and I are getting up there in age. Tried a few other openers but, this one works great. Wife weighs about 80 pounds and had no problem once she got the hang of how to place the jars in the jaws.
Easy to Mont. Just take the paper off the sticky stuff hold for about a minute. I drilled a pilot hold being carful not to drill through cabinet. Screws fit flush and did not go through base of cabinet.
Looks great. I waited a day before use to make sure the sticky stuff held.
First test was a salsa jaw that has defeated other openers. Most of the time I would end up using a knife to break the seal. Very little effort and it opened right up we other the new G grip.
I believe this is an American made product and it is made well
Very pleased with product and price.Easy to install and to use. When not needed it is totally unobtrusive. When needed it does what it is needed for. What more can one ask?The Grip Jar Opener is a game-changer! It easily opens jars of all sizes, from small caps to large lids, with zero effort. The under-cabinet design keeps it out of sight but always handy. It’s perfect for anyone who struggles with stubborn lids or has limited hand strength. Sturdy, versatile, and so easy to use—I wish I’d gotten it sooner!Really didn't expect this to be so effective. Easy to mount and easy to use. Works great to open all sizes of jars/bottles. Even my uber skeptical husband likes this and uses it. Saves me the frustration of trying to open tight jars/bottles with my hands that are weak from having had multiple carpel tunnel and trigger finger surgeries. Liked this so much I have bought 2 more for gifts.I had one of these mounted (out of sight) under our cabinet for years. Loved it and used it often.
In a recent kitchen renovation, I forgot to remove it and out it went along with our old cabinets.
I was so happy they still make it because it's honestly one of the most useful tools in my kitchen.
I'm not old, nor do I have arthritis but why struggle even a little with opening jars when this makes it (almost) effortless.
This opener works on almost every size jar or lid you throw at it . . . from metal olive jar lids to thick plastic caps on energy drinks. I don't even try to open jars myself anymore - - - usually I find I'm giving the jar a half-effort try as I head directly toward this opener.
There is nothing negative to say about it except that the teeth on it do mar the lid a bit. The teeth bite into the sides of the lid to hold it in place while you turn the jar/bottle, and that usually leaves marks on the sides of the lid/cap.
I don't typically save jars or bottles, but every once in a while I'll open a jar that I'd like to reuse later. On these occasions, putting a thin old rag between the teeth and the lid will still allow grip enough to open it initially, yet protect it from scratches so you can reuse it again.
Our old upper cabinets had a recessed bottom, so this was tucked up and completely out of sight. Our new cabinets have a flush bottom so if I mounted this under our new upper cabinets, it would be visible.
This is not ugly, but it's also not something I wanted visible in my new kitchen so I mounted it under a shelf (with clearance below it) in my pantry instead. Since the jar I'm opening typically comes out of the pantry anyway, this location works just as conveniently.
Super handy item that you'll wonder how you managed to live without.I bought mine in the mid 1970s and have moved it with me from house to house as I moved over the years. I could not open jars easily, and now that I am almost 80 years old, I could never open anything without it. The coolest thing I have discovered was that I could open a bottle of champagne, which was impossible before. I just was not strong enough. I gently put the cork in the V area of the opener, very slowly and gently turn the bottle until the cork starts to come out. I then open it by hand the rest of the way. I learned you have to do that very gently, but with a lot of upward pressure and the cork does not rip apart. I came across an article recently that directed me to Amazon....and am glad to see it is still being made...but just with a differnet name.I had a friend staying with me for several months after her husband passed away. She has arthritis in her hands and could never open bottles and jars. She was always asking for my help. Then I saw this handy little thing and I ordered one and put it under the kitchen counter. It made opening jars so easy for her that I decided to order a second one for her new home. Then I bought one for my sister-in-law because she was telling me she was starting to have trouble opening jars. She loves hers as well. A nice thing about it is it’s under the kitchen cabinet. You don’t even see it but when you need it is there.
reviews_1s:
It does states that installation is with adhesive but there is nothing on the back of the prodict and only screws in the box.The tooth blade came off the plastic housing after opening up around 2 dozen jars. Would not buy again,
I bought an all stainless steel opener, hopefully it will last longer.It has rust spots on it. Needless to say, I would pass on this if I were you.this will destroy the jar lid! makes deep cuts that will cut your handsI am extremely disappointed in this product. In spite of the advertisement showing a person using one hand to open a smooth metal jar lid, I have found it impossible to open a similar lid using two hands in trying to force the jar lid against the teeth. I have yet to find a metal lid that can be opened using The Grip Jar Opener. Trying to open metal lids which even have a rough edge, such as a canning jar lid doesn't work. The only lids that I have been able to open are plastic lids with a rough edge. Even some plastic lids with a rough edge will not open. All metal lids that I have tried and any smooth plastic lid and some plastic lids with a rough edge will simply slide on the metal teeth of the Grip Jar Opener and not open. I was very excited when this device arrived. I installed it and was immediately disappointed when I tried to open a lid.I almost never leave reviews, let alone bad ones.
But this just beat it all.
I received the product on time, well packaged and everything, even had a coupon for buying another one.
Install was a breeze, the glue pad holds under the kitchen cabinet without issues.

BUT THE DANG THING JUST DOESN'T WORK!

The premise seems ok, there are sharp teeth pointing in one direction to grip and hold the kid while you twist the jar open.
I tried again and again, and besides scratching the lid it didn't do a thing. Even jars I can easily open by hand wouldn't hold on this thing, just get scratched and slip.
My wife keeps laughing at me for buying useless gadgets...
Extremely disappointed!Great tool initially, but after a few months, started to crumble at the “v” until it completely fell apart! It was installed correctly and used correctly - just cheap plastic that couldn’t hold together with frequent use! Will be buying one that is all stainless.This products works as intended but creates jagged edges on the jar lid that cuts your hand when holding the lid to open/close. I had to throw the lid away and put a ziplock sandwich bag over the top of the jar to close it. I immediately started the return process. So disappointed.Received with no screws included. Contacted manufacturer who told me they would send replacement screws, which then turned into they were 'on the way', which then became 'oh we might be able to ship you some priority mail but it will be expensive for us' boo hoo. Then they suggest I return and repurchase the whole thing because there is a very small chance this will happen again. Wow, what a fuss over two screws, been two weeks now since i reported the missing items and still nothing! Product might be awesome but their customer service sucks.I was hoping this would really work for me but I do not have the hand strength to hold up the jar to the gripper while turning it so if you have a weak hand and cannot lift the jar to connect to the gripper it's just a waste of money such a sad thing.
